prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde were computed using DFT at the tier `standard` level (B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting), in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (PySCF backend). The calculation used the provided benzaldehyde.xyz geometry as a single point and computed the Mulliken partial charges for the neutral, cationic (+1, doublet), and anionic (−1, doublet) species. From these, the Fukui indices f⁺ (nucleophile attack susceptibility), f⁻ (electrophile attack susceptibility), f⁰ (average), and the dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) were derived for each atom. SCF converged for all three states. The most nucleophilic site (highest f⁻) is the carbonyl oxygen (O-1, f⁻ = 0.2384). The most electrophilic site (highest f⁺) is carbon-3 (ipso carbon of the benzene ring, f⁺ = 0.3994). The dual descriptor confirms oxygen as ambiphilic (positive, +0.1404) while hydrogens are nucleophilic (negative, ≈ −0.21). Charge conservation checks for f⁺ and f⁻ both summed to 1.0000, indicating converged N±1 states. Integrity status: trustworthy.</prose>
